prompt.text: Full codex-cron design spec lives at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-codex-cron-design.md (sha256 in prompt.sha256). The embedded prompt text is elided in this salvaged artifact to avoid transcription drift; the spec file is the authoritative source.

plan.title: codex-cron — durable governed cron scheduler for agent jobs
plan.goal: Build codex-cron, a standalone 2-crate Rust workspace (codex-cron-core pure logic + codex-cron-cli effects) implementing a durable, governed cron scheduler for agent jobs that shells out to the codex and ao2 binaries without touching the ao2 source tree. Deliver hermes-parity schedule semantics (interval/cron/once), an at-most-once no-drift no-burst tick engine guarded by a cross-process lock, pluggable codex/shell/ao2 executors, file + optional webhook delivery, a complete clap CLI, daemon + OS-service install, and prompt-injection refusal — building and running cross-OS on macOS, Linux, and Windows.
non_goals: Chat-platform delivery integrations (Telegram, Slack, Discord) are out of scope for v1.
SQLite/FTS5 session search and cross-session memory are deferred; per-run .md plus per-job runs.jsonl is sufficient.
Agent self-scheduling (a job creating its own follow-up jobs) is deferred.
Sandboxed execution backends (Docker/SSH/Modal) are out of scope; executors run as local child processes.
A web UI or TUI is out of scope for v1.

steps.rationale: Scaffold the 2-crate Cargo workspace (codex-cron-core library + codex-cron-cli binary) that all later steps build on, per design 3 and 14.1.
acceptance: create a Cargo workspace with codex-cron-core and codex-cron-cli crates
add the workspace Cargo.toml, rust-toolchain.toml, and .gitignore

steps.rationale: Implement hermes-parity schedule grammar parsing in codex-cron-core as pure, fake-clock-testable logic, per design 4 and 14.2.
acceptance: implement parse_duration accepting m, min, h, hour, d, day units with plural tolerance
implement parse_schedule resolving the interval, cron, and once kinds
verify all three schedule kinds and the error cases parse correctly

steps.rationale: Implement compute_next_run with the no-drift anchoring and single-catch-up fast-forward that are the scheduler's correctness backbone, per design 4 and 14.3.
acceptance: implement compute_next_run anchored to last_run_at so interval runs never drift
ensure a stale recurring job fast-forwards to a single future occurrence rather than a burst
verify the interval, cron, and once next-run semantics under a fake clock

steps.rationale: Define the Job data model and the versioned codex-cron.jobs.v1 envelope used by the store, per design 5 and 14.4.
acceptance: define the Job model and the versioned codex-cron.jobs.v1 store envelope with serde
verify round-trip serialization of the jobs envelope

steps.rationale: Define the Clock/JobStore/Executor/Delivery trait boundaries and the tick algorithm with advance-before-run persistence — the at-most-once correctness core, per design 3, 6, and 14.5.
acceptance: define the Clock, JobStore, Executor, and Delivery trait boundaries in core
implement the tick pass that advances next_run_at and persists before running each due job
verify at-most-once firing across a simulated mid-run crash with advanced state persisted
verify repeat exhaustion deletion, once completion deletion, paused and disabled skip, and the max_parallel cap

steps.rationale: Implement FileJobStore with atomic temp-fsync-rename writes so the schedule survives crashes and restarts, per design 6, 10, and 14.6.
acceptance: implement FileJobStore with atomic temp-fsync-rename writes and an in-process mutex
verify durability leaves no partial file and the store round-trips on disk

steps.rationale: Implement the cross-process advisory tick lock so the daemon and an external OS scheduler can both drive ticks safely, per design 6 and 14.7.
acceptance: implement the cross-process tick lock with fs2 held only for the select-advance-persist section
verify two concurrent ticks serialize and the second tick skips

steps.rationale: Implement the codex, shell, and ao2 child-process executors that capture stdout/stderr/exit status into a RunOutput, per design 7 and 14.8.
acceptance: implement the codex, shell, and ao2 executors as child-process spawners passing prompts via args or stdin
verify shell stdout capture and the wakeAgent false silent-run gate with a real sh invocation
verify the codex and ao2 executors against a fake on-PATH binary so CI needs no real binaries

steps.rationale: Implement the file (always-on) and optional webhook delivery targets, including bounded retry and SSRF allowlist guidance, per design 8 and 14.9.
acceptance: implement the file and webhook delivery targets with bounded webhook retry and backoff
verify per-run markdown output and a webhook POST against a mock HTTP server

steps.rationale: Implement the prompt-injection scanner and wire it into the tick before agent runs, since unattended runs auto-approve tools, per design 6.5, 11, and 14.10.
acceptance: implement the prompt-injection scanner over the assembled agent prompt
ensure a poisoned prompt is refused and the refusal is recorded as the run error before the codex executor runs

steps.rationale: Implement the complete clap CLI surface that exercises the store, lock, executors, and delivery, per design 9 and 14.11.
acceptance: implement the clap CLI add, list, show, edit, remove, pause, and resume commands
implement the run, tick, daemon, doctor, and config subcommands
verify the add to list to run to remove lifecycle against a temp home directory

steps.rationale: Implement the daemon tick loop and cross-OS service install/uninstall for launchd, systemd user units, and Windows Task Scheduler, per design 2, 9, and 14.12.
acceptance: implement the daemon loop and the install and uninstall paths for launchd, systemd user units, and Windows Task Scheduler
verify the generated service unit registers on the host OS

steps.rationale: Run the full verification gates and a smoke run, capturing evidence, per design 12 and 14.13.
acceptance: run the full workspace test suite and the clippy deny-warnings gate
verify codex-cron doctor reports healthy and capture smoke-run evidence

steps.rationale: Write the README covering install, quickstart, schedule grammar, executors, daemon/service, and config, per design 14.14.
acceptance: write the README covering install, quickstart, schedule grammar, executors, and config
document the daemon and OS service install flows

manual: review the generated launchd, systemd user, and Windows Task Scheduler units register on the host OS
confirm a stale recurring job fast-forwards to a single catch-up run rather than a burst after downtime

quality.salvage_note: Plan body authored by the sdd-planner claude provider (ao2 0.4.80, engine 2da7aed). The orchestrator's --out write was skipped because the claude CLI emitted a reasoning preamble before the JSON candidate and the provider parser requires pure JSON on stdout. The candidate was salvaged verbatim; only schema_version was corrected (candidate.v1 -> ao2.sdd-plan.v1) and the engine-owned placeholder strings (plan_id, generated_at_utc, prompt, target, provenance.engine_sha/cli_version) were filled post-hoc. ao2 source was not modified.
quality.paths_note: Every step.paths is the design-spec doc because sdd-planner validator rule V2 binds step.paths to surface_map.files[].path, and the greenfield target repo's surface contained only the committed spec doc at plan time. The real per-step file targets are in the design spec (sections 3 and 14); the implementation follows the spec.
quality.validation: Passes `ao2 sdd validate --plan` (no surface map; V2 path-binding not enforced).
